Notes: Some nice cu appeared on a seemingly poor day, so I squeezed in Hotel Alpha's favourite task.
Glorious thermals whenever lucky enough to stumble across one!
Hot, nauseous, diminished mental faculties. Decided to head home. On the other hand, getting to 7500ft was fun!

Another great day; easily the most stress-free flight so far. Thermals aplenty, all the way to 6000+.

FLARM still running with an old task, once again thankful I made a paper declaration too.
Cloud Rally task.
Is there a trophy for the slowest club 100? My first cross-country flight, thoroughly rewarding. It was all blue for the last leg but I just about managed to scrape my way back home.

Morning a little tricky due to (1) 800ft launch and (2) overcast bands moving down from the North. Afternoon was just great! Speed still geriatric due to conservative flying.

First 300k, silver & gold distance.
Not quite what I had in mind.
Tiptoeing around. Very, very lucky to make it back home.

Launched far too late - though not by choice. Great out to Thetford, but poor on the 2nd leg, sea air perhaps? Abandoned the task and went home whilst I still could. Conditions steadily improved moving inland.

Not sure if I can claim this, because I needed the engine to get back to re-start.  If not - for interest only!
Mostly blue.  West of Ely a few haze caps.  West of Northampton some small clouds.
Pity about the slow start. Pity I dropped the water just before the first decent thermal of the day.  Still, final 209km in 1:46 was rather good fun.
Late night last night, so a modest task with a late start.  Thermals in the east were tough to work - narrow and turbulent.  Best conditions west of Gransden.
Post-COVID rehabilitation flight
A bit of a fumble around Littleport, otherwise rather a romp.
A refresher in preparation for my Bronze exam tomorrow.

Skysight said there would be a convergence parallel to the north Norfolk coast - and indeed there was.  I've never soared such a front before and no doubt made many naive mistakes, but nevertheless it worked for long periods on the Oundle-Fakenham leg.  More difficult on the return to Upwood, but there I found a small patch of sea air - and south of it there was an excellent climb.  Enjoyed that too much, so a final glide at 120 knots wasn't sufficient to burn off the excess height.
Ran out of hope around Stratford.  8/8ths for a couple of hours or so wore me down.
Just a bit late to start.  So the sea air was already at Framlingham, and the 8/8ths at Banbury looked utterly hopeless.  Apart from that, really rather good fun.

A late start due to incompetence, fumbling and mechanical failure (the winch).
Not sure why this is so slow - although I found it very difficult to put runs together and I often had to deviate significantly to find good lift.  Very often, what seemed like a '6 knot thermal' turned out to average only 2 or 3 - they were very narrow and difficult to centre.  Some very good climbs out of nowhere and some sort of convergence got me home.

Task seemed to fit the weather reasonably well, although there was clearly a further 1-2 hours in the day.
I struggled at the start.  I seemed to do best when I forgot about trying to circle in the thermals and just ploughed on.

This felt a lot faster than 88kph.  A 66km run into wind from Bedford at 105kph (groundspeed) made me feel good.  The single 360 I threw at the end of it was a complete waste of time!
I found it very difficult to make the thermals deliver what they promised, and once again just pressing on through them seemed the best tactic.

So near, and yet so far - especially by road (many thanks to Andrew and Cathy for coming to get me).
I stuck with this task, knowing that the northern turns were forecast to be difficult, because I'd prepared it.  I was relieved to round Chesterfield without too many problems.  As I approached Sheffield for the second time conditions were excellent and I anticipated an easy run home.  But as I got closer I seemed to be running between problems on each side: showers, spreadout and some sort of convergence.  20km out the good stuff stopped.  10km short, everything stopped except the sink.  Wave down and then rain!
I last flew more than 590km in 1996 - must try harder!
Slow start for cloudbase to rise then very good conditions with cloudbase rising to 4500
Very broken thermals between SIL and BAN, felt like wave interference, rest fairly straightforward, but clouded up rapidly after landing
Slow to get going, but i hoped it would keep going later, and it did
Originally set Andover but was in a huge area of blue so decided to do a club task of BUG O&R, good to the west early on but huge spreadout on the way back
lots of cloud, lots of weak climbs
What a grovel, lots of low points, luckily wind was light and lots of thermals, base eventually reached 4000 on the way home at Northampton
Ran out of climbs, hoped it might work without detouring, it didnt
Frustrating at times trying to work out the thermals, best was at the end with 5000 ft cloudbase and round thermals
Well set task by Mike Smith!, some long glides in the blue but with base nearly 6000ft no problem
Good climbs to 4K later, bit underdeclared.
Good all the way, one climb averaged 11.2, also some long glides, but no spreadout!
Skysight spot on, clouds to start then blue and perfect cu again from Scunthorpe to the turn , blue again with occasional wisps all the way home.
abandoned due showers
Spreadout on the first leg, but conditions rapidly improved, base 5K in the west with strong climbs , and sink, lighter winds so thermals better shaped.

Brilliant in the east to start, ok to about EDG when spreadout started, good embedded cu anyway and base was now 5k, bit of a struggle all the way back till Bedford when it opened up and base now rose to over 6K.
This year keeps getting better!, perfect cu almost the whole way round and 6K cloudbase in places.
difficult
slow to get going then from Peterboro on base 4-5K with very good climbs mostly, slightly soft near the wash, Marham closed Cambridge closed, great

Fantastic conditions all the way until it stopped !!! Sad really, we would have made it if conditions had remained as they were ,, had enough now , taking up gardening!  Thanks Peter for your happy company and great flying!

An interesting day out, Becky and Andy flying. 1st leg was ok until the murk appeared and then vis was atrocious, however we were JUST short of 7000' at one point. 2nd leg was much faster and the TP came up fairly quickly, then near the 3rd TP the day was ending. A climb just outside Ramsey with Upwood in sight saved the day. Uneventful final glide to GRL.
Well, great day ,certainly my best this season,  ably helped by Stephen !!
Hard work,, getting old ,, Thanks Tony Pledger for your great assistance !!

Another truly epic day which i tried to mess up by going too fast ,!! Beautiful conditions with epic visibility, especially around the wash  Thanks Mark for your great help ,especially with the hard bits !!
Great flight with Harry aftet two years away ,welcome back !!
Flew with David Williams, the new course instructor just to show him a circuit,,,, difficult in places, and surprisingly easy in othet places, good fun day out !!
Lovel day out with the club secretary to Newark and back. A bit low and slow to start with but after Wittering things got better and better, Thanks to Jonathan for all your help.

Beautiful day to start, epic visibility and good climbs, 8 knots in places,Very strong wind made life interesting below 1200 feet,,, couple of low struggles but otherwise very easy ! Another good day out with Mark !!!
Great flight with James Kellerman, got the last bit badly wrong by tryiing to keep up with Wendy and David ,,,bad idea !!             Thanks to Mike Smith for a great task !!

Interesting (and difficult in place) day out with Matija, really good in places and quite painful in others (broken thermals and general incompetence),,,Brize Norton lured us in with clearance for zone transit only to close the church doors when we where on the steps of the church!!!  Had to divert hundreds of miles so very slow today !!!

Just putting this on to make Wendy's flight look even better !!
I just couldn't get through the dead looking air 50 k short of Belvoir  ,,

One of those (many) days that looked rubbish until about 12, ,then looked good enough for a 200 k.flight .. of course at the top of the winch it looked like a 400 k day !! 
Lovely flight round the countryside with Joanne Brand with great visibility and a pilot who knows how to thermal !!

Shakedown flight , testing my garmin gps xl etc ,all worked well !
Thanks to whoever set the task,perfect for me !
Lovely day out really with the ever capable and quiet Duncan, thanks for the help !!

Well, there's always some schmuck who tries to go really fast and ends up going really slow  ,, oh well , there's always next year !! Apart from the speed , had a great flight with Steve ,, thanks for the great company and eyesight  !!

Great flight, first time visiting the Mynd by glider. Too slow to get the best out of the day , blue for the last 100 k so decided to land at Shennington and take an aerotow back . Good to fly with Mark, might take him again !!

Thanks to the task setters for a great task, unfortunately too old and late to take advantage of it all and left out the last turn point ! Nevertheless, a great day out with the lovely Steve,  and beautiful views down the valleys near Hay Bluff!!

Strange day ,soggy to start and rhen five knots from nowhere,,steady improvement, but looked iffy in the last leg to Hereford cathedral so bottled out and came home ! Lovely day out with James Kellerman!!
Camilla's first cross country ,excellent time had by both of us ,,,relief !!!
Got round !!! Excellent flight with Mark from Australia,,,lots of 5 knots ,a few 7.5 knots and great streets, also great flying from Mark ,, best day for a while !!

Well, faffed around badly off the winch launch so was always playing catch up with the weather,,even the easy bit seemed quite difficult and then we got to Milton Keynes on the way back where it had turned blue .Half an hour later we really hadn't got anywhere but we were so slow the sky decided to re cycle and we managed to scrape along to a cumulus which helped us to the next one and so on,,, really relieved to get home .. The star of the show was Lorna who i hardly let fly and sat there for hours and hours without complaining, bless her !!
Becky and I, lovely day out with great streets and wonderful visibility!
One of those best flights ever"
column87: got so high one of my tooth filligs started to leak from an air pocket !!  Outrageous conditions

Notes: Well, we all set the task just before launch, 25 minutes later I’m at the first turn point, Bury, in horrendous conditions, wondering  how I’m going to get back and then I hear on the radio my mates ?? have decided to abandon the task and do a club task !!  Eventually ,having spent far too long visiting and re-visiting Bury, i managed to crawl back to Grl ,, saw better conditions to the west of st neots, and like true troopers, Nigel and decided to stick to the task and battle on,,, actually lovely conditions for the rest of the flight !

Where were you all ?? No fun trying to beat myself!! Actually it was still fun with the one and only lovely Lorna, albeit it with very difficult thermals 80% of the time.. No real problems at all getting round, just struggled to centre the retched things ,,,,, Was a bit lonely today, almost missed you guys, (and girls)....

Flew with Stephen Goss , fortunately for me an expert thermaller !!
Turned back early because of the sea breeze,  good but tiring flight !!
Another lovely day, good to fly with Lorna Sleigh, best possible task set by the legendry David Tew, many thanks!!
Well,another great flight ,,all blue after Silverstone and strong wind ,,again.          Thanks Mark for your company and theoretical conversation!!

Well,,, obviously underset but don't care!! Fabulous flight with the best conditions in years , 8 knot thermals and fantastic visibility,  superb views along the coast from Blakeney point,,,and fast for us !! Thanks Mark, good company and great flying !!
2nd 500 - last 20 years ago! Also visited the seaside - twice - which I've not done before. Always been something I wanted to do.
Late start, difficult. Declared  GRM but with the time I started (and my skill!), it wasn't going to happen.

I believed the forecast (or my interpretation ) and came out too late. Concerned about the early showers (!!!) I set a modest" task - and still went slow. This time due to getting stuck at Banbury on the way out (and my lack of skill of course)."
The points don't reflect the effort required to achieve this!
Bit slow (a usual!). Couple of low points which eat a bit of time. Some bits really fun!

Day was better than the forecasts would have me believe. Joined in with the triangle (suggested by Robert T I believe). Bit blue in places and I found it hard to center on the good bits most of the time - but I guess that's just me!
Cloud Rally - slow going on first leg, weather improved after that (but I'm still slow!)
A good task from George, although I didn't manage much speed. Framlingham to Downham started well - a good line of energy, but generally I found the into wind legs hard going!
ICL started well but clouded over and finally had to light up the FES north of Cranfield on the way back.

Flight resubmitted owing to time error first time (I thought that it was fast for me!). Missed the last tp by 9km due to sea air and no lift, got stuck for a while near Cambridge. I'll go faster one day!

Today was easier than yesterday, achieved task but scored less than yesterday's. RB gave me today, I didn't think it would be better than yesterday, but many others thought it would. So when I arrived (late at the time I thought would give me the best launch time) loads of others there already launching and setting off on 500s. Oh well, at least I achieved what I declared!
Dunstable ICL
Dunstable ICL

Skysight showed the winds as having moderated from earlier forecasts - turned out not to be the case! Highest wind noticed was 32kts. I gave up on my declared task on the way to LYV (first t/p), decided that I'd get that far then come home via Thrapston. I did get back to near Offord before I started the motor, but the system doesn't allow a grid point as completion of an undeclared task. I was seconds away from starting the motor earlier just after Thrapston, but ran into a reasonable" climb.  High wave setting up by this time (but not found lower down). Just posting this to remind myself of the folly of high winds on blue days (but I did find a blue street at one point)."

Hmm, forgot/ didn't realise that you can only have four tps - the task was actually 434km, but taking out Rattlesden airfield reduced it a bit. Got a bit interesting up by the Wash when the sea breeze front was trying to get started and also in the blue later, but I got round albeit at my usual speed!

Well, nobody else thought it worthwhile (again!). I was just very happy to get round, never mind the speed! Several versions of plan B considered at various points. Best climb of the day over the Addenbrookes estate (Cambridge) on the way home - 4.9kts average for the thermal. Took it way above what was required (who wouldn't?) and finished at 100+kts.
Slow, windy
An outing in a Club glider whilst mine is not available - just to alleviate the looking at the sky and not flying" frustration!"
Took a punt on the weather (forecast poor) but as it turned out, should have gone further...

A masterclass of indecision... didn't like the look of the blue flat sky to the west so went for local soaring but then the conditions improved so decided to try for some points with a Rushden and back...

Only had a couple of hours for gliding today but managed to declare and squeeze in the Club 100 this afternoon. Chuffed to bits to fly in stunning soaring conditions, most enjoyable. Thanks to the GRL team for my speedy getaway before the break. Hopefully will now have enough domestic points for a full day tomorrow :)

Went conservative today after my last two local flights ended scruffily in the GRL circuit. Thought I'd try to do something disciplined and confidence building. Originally declared GRL-Downham Market-Bicester-GRL (c.260k) but the bluish sky to the east looked like it was above my skill level so I floated about, had lunch and then (when the clouds to the west built a bit) did the Club 100. Great fun and my circuit to land was far less interesting" than the last two attempts :)

Season: A good confidence boost for next time..."

Notes: Having watched you lot fly on Friday the 5th (whilst I was at work) I was determined to have fun today... lots of blue west of Edgehill... my first 500... chuffed to bits...

Took 40kgs of water... it was suggested I should have taken more... I should've taken more!
My other challenge at the club-hut was to finish at more than 80 kph (handicapped was not specified so that's a win!!)... getting faster slowly (if that makes sense).
Second half of leg 2 was a bit flat for my liking but the towns supplied loads of lift...
My 4th 300k+ :)

Should I have attempted a 400k? The friendly banter at the launch point suggested yes but decided to stick to the 360k (hey, my first 350k+ :)
Once again got shown how to do it by being overtaken by (in turn) J1, 899, 871 and 700... I must stop being nervous about shoving the nose down and pushing on... although the last leg required a bit of care... ironically once low on final glide the vario was going mad with lift... wish I'd known that before!!! 
Saw an interesting medium level cloud... not sure what it was but very pretty...

Blue... no white at all... a hint of over-confidence in my first full blue experience ended quite quickly with a restart (Bourn airfield looks lovely from that height). After that it was another master-class of indecision and the original Newmarket-Oundle-Bicester plan got shelved for something slightly more manageable... hilariously good fun and the gliding experience keeps on giving...

A tip-toe" of an afternoon... the clue should have been the lack of trailer park activity at GRL... but still great fun. Another day of beautiful high level cloud but not much low level stuff to look at..."
Could Messrs Beatty and Linger not overtake quite so convincingly next time please?
Was a go flying and see what happens" sort of flight... the high speed final glide was because I realised I should have been at home taking the kids to an after school activity... it worked..."

Confidently set off but soon thwarted by a massive blue gap west of Bedford. Decided to aeronautically park over Olney (last week I parked near Milton Keynes, Olney is more picturesque) until the clouds rolled in from the west. Then set off again and enjoyed watching some of GRL's finest overtake me westbound... found the day quite difficult but strangely enjoyable. Used Edgehill and then Turweston as my hopping points on the way home (whilst listening out on Turweston heard a glider join and land... gave me some encouragement not to join him!)
Had a few issues with my electronic vario which decided to go haywire a few times so reverted to just using the basic vario... seemed beautifully simple... 
Also tried some ballast but apart from watering some grass in Warwickshire, not sure I gained much from it... all in all a fun day out!!

Turned up early ready for the big one... then the sky went very grey so I went for a local float around taking in some local sights" (turnpoints)... totally undeclared and took in Oakington and Papworth as well as the TP's mentioned. Good fun but didn't feel like a day to get low...."

My first flight from Denbigh… expected a local hour but conditions better than forecast. Hence conjured up an undeclared mini-task to keep me amused without venturing too far from the airfield (and trickles me over 4000 km for the year :)

Cloud Rally day 7... only managed one day of it :( 
Next year!!
Got a bit blue heading north at Watton (North Sea air?) so bottled out and headed home...

Declared my first 500... did the first half but it seemed to take forever. At St Neots (half way) I'd run out of energy (personal not aeronautical). Huge huge respect for Julia who had the perseverance to continue back to the west at a late stage of the day and complete the task...

Enjoyed the first leg and the first half of the second leg and then the sky went flat and grey like a newly laid factory floor. Looked far too tricky for my skill level so I bottled out and turned for home... still great fun though. Lakenheath were superb and totally unfazed by me thermalling amongst their KC135's and F35's...
What a hoot...

My first visit to Denbigh... huge thanks to Chris and everyone there for being so unbelievably helpful and welcoming, including all the folks from Saltby. 
Day 1: thermals.
Day 2: ridge (until the wind stopped or was it me? :) 
Day 3: wave.
Today had the pleasure of flying with Chris in the Arcus who showed me the ropes with wave... then climbed into my LS6 and enjoyed my first wave solo. Met up with Chris and Roger in the Arcus to take some snaps... hilariously good fun. I'll be back!!

Set off for Sleaford, Grafham Water, Donington but then realised that 300k+ was a bit optimistic when it went grey north of Peterborough. Settled on a smaller version... 
Thermalling going south was a dream but gaining height on the north bound legs was a bit of a challenge... one step forward, two back :) Hence the faff trying to round Ramsey...

How lucky are we to do this stuff...
This was my 2nd 300k XC... wahoo!! Told you it wasn't a fluke :)
Thanks to Bogdan for the suggestion...

Thanks to CU for the suggested task...
Great day out... I still think its amazing that you can have this much fun without using loads of avgas!
This gliding lark is bloody good fun... you never really know what's going to happen. Optimism outstripped ability today but I learned a lot...
Hoped to do my first 300.  The day started late and cut to 205k
My first completed 300k! Chose a very good day
Gold height
Late submission to FastTrack ladder
Diamon Goal (300k triangle). Couldn't have wished for better conditions!  Great fun
Faster at second attempt
Tough day, but got round
Thoroughly enjoyed the flight.  300k was too far!
Tough!
Landed out in a potato field.  Deep ruts.  Many thanks to the retrieve crew!
Went for 300k.  Doing well until the thermals disappeared!  This was still my longest flight by some margin
Inter club league - landed out at Main Hall Farm
A storming day getting over 6,000 climbs.  Getting faster
Chickened out when clouds disappeared
A tough day at the cloud rally!
My longest flight (mainly) in the blue - character forming" when cirrus cut everything off - landed at Lyveden. Coningsby and Waddington were very helpful with MATZ penetration requests."
Bit of a struggle to Norman Cross , worse under clague to Banbury - but the run home made it all worthwhile
First completed X/C of the season so glad to get round: pity I couldn't fly the first two legs as quickly as the third -

Looked pretty rubbish at Bedford, but kept with the task I'd planned anyway. Andy and Lorna were delightfully helpful for marking the lift though! Great day - fastest and longest task I've done to date. Well done James for being a couple kph faster..!

Got a bit stuck in the gloom over Bury, trying to find climbs under big CU. Should have pushed on more to make it to the better climbs in the blue. The run back from Newmarket was good, other than almost missing the last control point...
It's amazing what you can do when you put the nose down... First flight over 100kph
Quick thrash around today's task with David! I got a bit carried away racing him on final glide... but still climbed away and tried the task again! Another great day with a dreadful forecast!
Tricky run along the edge of the clag up to Crowland and a low point at Kings Lynn. Tough decision making and hugely rewarding, exactly what I'm here for! Great fun racing Mark and Aiden around.

Pretty classic day, again much better than forecast. My head wasn't screwed on right to start with, but being 700ft the other side of Cambridge really focuses one's mind... Climbed away, came home, started again. Learnt some useful lessons today.
Why stop when you're having fun?
Spent the first half thinking I'd underset and the second half thinking I'd overset... Good day, run back from Bicester was tough and tanked my speed.

Task is best match for what we were set in the briefing. Weather didn't work out as expected - it seems being at the back of the grid worked to my advantage today... Made big detours for good clouds, and it seems to have paid off today.
Rather pleased and buckets of fun!
Tricky. Lots of clouds didn't seem to work as expected. I suspect the narrow cores made it possible to miss the lift under good clouds. Made it round though - good fun chasing Phil and Anne in PZ!

Last day of the Cloud Rally. Fairly uneventful. Got some epic climbs, but couldn't seem to trace particularly good lines between and along the streets. Thank you to everyone at Gransden for a brilliant week!

Was blue for most of it, with haze caps working up to >7000ft. Slow outbound into wind. Made a few mistakes that buggered up the final glide: first, didn't set up my flight computer properly; second, when I turned to get out of a line of sink, I didn't turn back onto task... Landed out 5km from the airfield. Lessons learnt for next time. Pints owed to Ollie and Charlie H for the retrieve - we almost made it out of the field before dark...

First thermic flight of the year, great to be back in the air. Didn't plan to go XC, but looked good west so tried the club 100. Looked rubbish between Ramsey and GRL, so packed it in rather than taking the risk. Should have kept going west!
First 300k. Flying from Saltby for the Interunis task week. Enormous thanks to Andrew for briefing and task setting - and to Harry for the encouraging optimism.

Was meant to be on BI duty, but there were no trial flights. Thankfully there was a glider available! It was very tough making any ground to windward but perseverance was eventually rewarded.

(I can't figure out which settings to use to get the variable barrels to calculate and display correctly on here: it was meant to be a 6.1km (variable) barrel for the Junior.)
CGC Cloud Rally Day 4
First solo cross-country flight and my first flight of the CGC Cloud Rally.     : )
CGC Cloud Rally Day 8. Great conditions so I kept pushing on at 90kts which ended up with me picking fields then HGV found the thermal that got me home. : )
CGC Cloud Rally Day 7
The extra weight in the back slowed me down. Or it might have been the combination of glider and headwind. Either way it was great fun.
CGC Cloud Rally Day 5
CGC Cloud Rally Day 3
208 km declared.  Slow round the first TP, hence 64 kph.  Good fun though.  The weather looked better than it actually was!
Easy out to Ely, then gave up on the way to Turweston due to being a bit cowardly and the lure of tea and biscuits.
Had no retrieve crew, so wasn't allowed to cut the umbilical cord, so went round in small circles for 4 hours!
Heading for Husbands Bosworth.  Got as far as Kettering.

It's bad when it's SO windy (27 kts at times) that one has trouble overtaking an airfield!  Which is what happened on the way back from Hunstanton lighthouse.  Downwind (going north) was easy.  Coming back upwind (twice) was a real struggle.  The streets weren't really working that well, so it was more a very broken, difficult to centre, cu day.  Challenging, but v satisfying!
Not much to tell.  Flew upwind until I came across an extensive rain squall.  Then turned round and went home!
277 km declared (Gransden - Tibenham-Crowley A/F - Gransden).  A bit more flown, because I got back earlier than expected.  6-8 kt climbs were common.
It was tricky.

David refused to fly until we were over 3500', so I did the blue scrappy start - after Peterborough, the thermals switched on and we shared the rest of the flight for a romp round. Task seemed spot on for the day, after 2pm it was really nice and the approaching frontal high cover didn't spoil things, still heaving after 5pm. So probably should have started about 20mins later, but still good fun!
Interesting sea breeze effect beyond Tib.

declared HEC OR but had a pressing engagement at 6 pm with Maverick
so abandoned at TEW due to the murk ahead
got back in time to pack up, change and eat ,,,, all before pulling ludicrous amounts of cinema G in an F 18 !!

Not a bad day, gym, a few hours work and then an afternoon 300K!
Thermals were scrappy on the way out with very small tight cores (when you could find them) - as per normal, I got to do the hard start, then David took over when it got better!
After Edgehill, although there was sun on the ground, there was significant spread out and a lower cloudbase.  But worked OK, especially after a divert before the turn.
On the way back the high cover was switching things off and we struggled a bit with the final climb onto glide, but then the sun came out at MK and Bedford and it was booming!
Glad I ignored the suggestions at Shipston that we turn back!!
Very nice flight and good fun sharing the flying.

LX lost the task, so flew on the Oudie and re-assigned task to logger file.

Well, what a day! We found it quite hard going out, things only started going after Milton Keynes. Then a good run into Didcot.  On the second leg, high cover was encroaching from the West and there was spread out, so we were cautious into Edgehill and out.
We had the 'Bemuda Triangle' effect again near Towcester. But after that, good run home.

So, in the end very slow, but hadn't been sure we would fly at all, so was nice to do and complete a task.

George's odd-sounding task, but Lakenheath closed for the US holiday. Turned out great. I need to work on speed in the new toy, and I definitely should have had water on board, but 1st 300 in new glider.

Late start as waiting for conditions to improve beyond dreadful. Well... when she was good she was very, very good, when she was bad she was horrid. Under blue sky and clouds it was great, under total spread out and overdevelopment, it was torrid. Faster than bicycle, but still pretty pedestrian overall. However, on a day most people didn't go cross-countrey, I'm pleased enough.
Inter-Club League. Occasional reasonable climbs but horrid, low and either overdeveloped or cut off by top cover, and speed such that task could not be completed in the window of soarable conditions.

Either started too late or too early. For first hour local, could only just stay airborne, then went very slowly to BED, then better on return to Halesworth and stormed back straight into a hole near BSE that took ages to climb out of.
Second flight in new glider. :-) Delayed start (thanks LX) so fell back to BIC O/R undeclared. Massive underset.

Continuing the quicker on a bike theme..., but this time actually completed a task with no engine (after the recent spate of failures). Had to be home early so set small and set off in crap. By final glide the day had turned really nice!

Skysight said there was an early, *fast* 200 to be done in this area. I proved it wrong. Horrible firtsl leg, nearly had engine out... Better second leg at first then died short of Peterborough. Not a single steady, round thermal to be had from start to the thermal I found after making final glide. Wave affected? Every one fought me, odd shapes, ill-defined centres etc. Robert Theil said I should have done it the other way around, but I don't think it would have made much difference.

Well... First thermal 3 knots, second 7 knots. Go for start! All went wrong, grovelled back for relight. Glider had sat wing down so water dribbled out vent. Relight nearly cartwheeled into crop, so dumped all water, fell back to smaller task and went. Very indifferent conditions. Blue North of Bicester, but I'm OK with blue, so initially not bothered, but frankly nothing much seemed to work, and had a torrid time of it.

Should have followed the boys up the Great Convergence Line, clearly...
Nice day out...

Almost entirely horrible. Set off under 3000', a few decent thermals just to fool you, but mostly weak and broken. Turned Calvert after a leg one could probably do faster on a bicycle, then horrible gap, ending with engine start by Northampton

Apologies to Ollie Bosanko for suggesting the task (and he completed it, so well done him).

(Calvert now an HS2 bulding site!!!)

Robert Theil's task. By the time I got to Medlesham it was in sea air. 15k straight glide to turn and back again to real air slowed me down, then soft around Wisbech to Spalding, so I was much slower than I should have been. But, a glorious day, mostly spent above 4000, and at times nearly 7000 feet.

O/R to HUS with a kink at Lyvden to avoid Sywell. Some good thermals to 5000' but some were definitely ungentlemanly in their behavior. The low point of the way out was Corby and it was a black hole on the way back so used the motor. 90 secs later there was a thermal which gave me 5000' and a fast glide home. PS Just adding this flight to make Andy's look better!
Underset for the day of course, but a nice if hot trip as it got bluer and bluer.

It started well with strong thermals to 4000' despite the unpromising sky. Good run to RAM but cloud cover increased and thermals weakened and had to creep to RUS. Worse at RUS and then staggered back to GRL.

The plan was climb to 6000' and start for Ramsey, climb to 6000' again at Ramsey and Rushden for a record speed (for me) round the task. The thermals often seemed to promise a lot but delivered less and I never got above 5000'. Eventually set off using Caxton Gibbet as the start but messed up some of the climbs and the task speed was not what it could have been. Still met the 3-line whip and was home on time.

A fantastic unexpected day. It started with strongish thermals but turning STN and heading towards KLY it became clear that you could head into wind and find what at times seemed like a continuous stream of upward moving bubbles losing little height in the process, and the same on much of the return trip even under the high cover. Back at GRL the thermals were still v strong so I u-turned and added Ramsey and Rushden to the tour. Great day.

Going north was not the best decision. Set off in a largely blue sky with decent thermals to 4000'. By Crowland A/F thermals were weaker and the sky close to 8/8. Turning Colsterworth the route to Newark looked worse so abandoned the task. Heading home via Saltby and Lyveden was slow with weakening thermals. Eventually gave up just past Lyveden and started up the motor. But nice to visit 4 other GCs in one flight!

Declared GRL-MEN-HUS in the hope of getting my 300k. Was so cautious that it took all day to get to MEN and back!
But was first XC in the blue and is the furthest I've been so far, so not disappointed.
undeclared flight following the weather after my original task proved to be a bit ambitious.
Amazing conditions .
Shared flight with Julie Garside
Shame about the blue bits !
My first solo cross country in 20 years to the week.  Went blue after ST Neots.

Because of poor forecast for GRL area, I came out to work on the glider, but the general enthusiasm at the trailers caused me to rig instead. In the end, it turned out pretty much as forecast, but still quite reasonable. Maybe the cold air helped. About 8-10kts northerly, low cloudbase for much of the first leg up to about Oxford, then getting better. Tricky patch on the second leg around Swindon. Coming back towards GRL it looked increasingly dire (little sun on the ground) from about Bicester, but the odd thermal appeared when needed to get onto final glide.

Westerly at 20-30 kph. Original declaration was a triangle to the SW, but that was just beyond me, because going into wind from GRL the sky became grey and beyond Bedford basically featureless. Turning into the east, however, conditions improved markedly, and I had my first excursion over the North Sea. A second attempt at pushing west later on was a bit better, some sun was now getting through, but my motivation to push on into somewhat unreliable conditions had suffered during the rather tricky flight.
First attempt at 200km. A great day, but tricky around Hus Bos.. Didn't get too low but not brave enough to press on!
cloud rally

Started a bit too late and it took me an hour and a half to start as I couldn't get high in the blue, but then got brave enough to try to connect with the Cu.
Arrived at Mendlesham after the sea breeze front had passed it, but I was high so dived through the bad air and got round the turnpoint and back to the convergence..
A great flight in the end! Lost slightly over 1000m....
A really hard day in the blue.. Struggled all the way round and was pleased to get back without landing out even if it was really slow!

Cloud Rally Task-Tuesday..

Launched second and headed off early.. Very tricky especially around Sackville. Some amazing climbs round Ely, but lots were strong but very small and hard to centre..

Glad to get round even if not fast..

The forecast said it would turn blue, so I chickened out of going further afield.. In the end conditions were pretty good with some really strong climbs. 

Should have been faster but a good start to the season very late!

Great around GRL, but got tricky going W after Bedford - thermals not reliable and had a low spot at Silverstone - held on to the water and things got better after that to Shipston. But only 70 kph for that leg.
Fabulous run to Framingham, making the overall speed to 99 kph. Thermals more reliable, some spread out, but OK - down wind also helped!
On the way back, struggled to get a decent climb onto glide, and then after I did, managed to loose it all and had to land straight in (hence the height loss penalty)
During the flight oscillated between thinking it was overset and underset - in the end, think it was just about right. Not as much as a grovel back as some recent flights!

Almost without incident, but had a slight scrabble after Calvert, spent too long in the gusty part before finding the strong core, but down to 2000 agl, so didn't want to miss. Otherwise, strong climbs to over 5K - wisps, cu and then blue.
Pleasant afternoon jaunt!

Hard work on second leg with climbs only 2 knots to 3500' - maybe dampened down by band of cirrus before?
Middle of the country better, but not as good as yesterday.
Still, 3 days flying in a row, 1200K and 2 decent weekend ladder scores, that I needed!

Insane conditions - very stressful avoiding airspace.

Should take the UK Feminine 300k O/R for Open and 20m classes at 122.86kph (was 107.07 kph)

Slow to get going and tricky on the way out - nearly dumped the water at Westcott - better after that, but often found it hard to find the very good, narrow cores.
Clearance from Brize helped the second leg. Good run back.
Nice day out

Started early as wanted to be back before spread out, hence leg to Wisbech was a bit soft, but good climb at the TP, then reasonable run which just got better and better. Africa in the UK! Strong climbs and going to over 6k.  But did meet the spread out around Calvert on the way back, so slowed a bit there - but as ever good sunshine at Bedford to get me on glide. 

Thanks to 899 for the task - spot on for the day! We'd agreed E first then W and back by 4, and this task fitted the bill.

Modest start of season shake-down cross country - 100K in before I managed to properly core a thermal - murky to start at GRL, but then got going quite nicely with 3-4 knots to over 4000' (when you could find it!). Returning to the East easy as wind died and over development didn't happen.

Generally very good climbs - often 4-6knots, but sometimes had to settle for less. Found it scrappy between Didcot and Membury - not sure why, but often have problems there and not helped by higher ground which is bumpy and pilot bruised after Sunday's flight.

Lovely sight-seeing trip and great help from Yeovilton, and Bristol - nice day out - must get better at taking pictures! and must get faster!

Another hard work flight! We weren't in the best place in the country today!
Good run to Mendlesham and back - started with nice cu and ended with spread out. Pretty sure the task wasn't on, but headed out to the West and things improved after Bedford, strange cu, but working and sun on the ground.
Started to spread out again at Great Malvern - could see a way into BUG, but was worried about getting back. As it was it was survival from Banbury back, but sun at Banbury got me high and then small sun spots sun at MK and Bedford got me onto glide in an otherwise very flat sky.
Another crazy time!  
Well done to KPG and 64 to got round the whole flight.

Task speed was 92 kph to where I abandoned, it was the journey back that messed things up!

So, the plan was for a later arrival at the club after gym and chores and an afternoon 300k. But it looked better then expected, so I upped the task - maybe I should have stuck with my original plan!

Good run to Mendlesham under cu with 5 knot climbs and base getting up to 4K.  Reasonable up to Sleaford, although I messed up just before and had a low spot, but thankfully didn't need to dump the water.  Back down to RAT it was blueing out, although still cu, but not as strong and often dead by the time you got to them.  Last leg was a bit of a struggle and lost 2000' arrival height and had to land straight in, hence small height penalty.

But better then Mondays 400k!

Lakenheath and Cambridge very helpful

turned back 25K from Newtown as didn't like the look of the showers building - had a squeaky glide back into wind over the higher ground and popped over at the end of Lake Bala for a climb up - then went down to Caldair Idris, and played around on the way back - so effectively task was abandoned, but easiest thing to reference against.

Nice climbs, a bit of a slog back into 24+ knots wind and didn't like the cross wind landing, but nice day out!

168k on OLC distance

Lovely day out, lots of airspace dodging on the way back!

Thanks to 72 for the task, 64 for the mega thermal before GRM and for 28 for being hot on my heels and making me go faster!!

Not often you get over 7000' in thermals in the UK!

Well, that was very hard work.
Left at 3000' heading down wind with very broken thermals, very uncomfortable below 2000' and cloud base came down as well. Considered throwing the towel in, but given the plan was to return when the conditions got better that wasn't an option!
Improved after Towcester and then continued to improve as I went West - bases got to just over 4000' and climbs 3-5/7 knots.
all nice until heading back home after Towcester - very clear Wash sea-air incursion - just managed to make it to the cu at Bedford and then reasonable run in.

Enjoyed having M8 for company to chat to and hats off to 871 who made it back well over an hour later !

so, well deserved G&T now and hoping for some easier days!

OMG what a day!
good run to Framingham, but hit the front and gloom spread as I got back to GRL - dithered a lot at Bedford, and initially started to abandon, but being a nutter, I didn't want to give up, so I hung around, and then I saw sunshine coming through after the front, so I continued.  To Banbury, even though it was working, it was a bit scrappy and I got low a few times - they are starting to cut field to the West!
At Banbury, I had a nightmare - although downwind out, I could see nothing I to go for, so ended up pushing more into wind for something which eventually worked, but only after I dug myself out of an massive hole - I nearly started the engine out of frustration!
Run back after that was good, in fact so good that I got a massive climb at Poddington and decided to continue past GRL.  Had a nice master class from Chris Gill in LEW on running streets.  At 5:30, I was pleased I was at Newmarket with only GRL to get back to!
Abandoned 50k from Men and flew back.
Slowest flight ever, but my own Mini Competition Enterprise with dogged determination!

A bit of everything - booming around GRL - Blue-ing out after Westcott, into and out of Didcot - reasonable run to Pickenham, but then spread out hit on the way back for last 45K.  Was very conservative on final glide after last weeks marginal glide in. In the end was all OK.
The lesson today was that regardless of the conditions, only 1 out of 3 things I tried worked - once I sussed this, I tried to push on where I could.
Wasn't expecting to fly today, so a nice bonus.

Excellent in places, less good elsewhere. First 50k very broken scrappy thermals and cross wind made it hard. Picked up after that into Didcot and half way on second leg. Hus Bos usual hole and showers and big fire near Newark. Awesome leg down to Garbaldisham and reasonable run back.

Nice day out

Not as fast as I would have liked, but looks to beat current UK Feminine 20m and Open 500k Triangle speed

Not the day we had hoped with - should have stuck with just DEV O/R and not added on another 30k, although it was very nice thermalling over Westonbirt Arboretum.

The occasional good climb of 5 knots, but many 2-3 knots and a bit scrappy. Got low near Cranfield on the way-out, but after that OK into the 2 TPs and out until after Oxford. Then had to dig myself out of a hole and was loosing the will to live and getting slower. A climb at Westcott helped, but failed to get anything at MK - I think I was getting tired. Cranfield very helpful and let me try to get away in their ATZ, in the end pushed the go home switch"
File created on: and then went into a thermal!!!   V11 proved it was possible to get home - well done!"

Notes:
Sack the task setter - 25K into and out of Framingham in sea air made it a bit squeaky.
Apart from that, very nice conditions and best flight so far this year.
Marham and Norwich very helpful with clearances.

One of those days when you remember why you fly! 

Lovely once it got going - reliable 3-5 knot climbs to over 4000' and good streeting. Probably my last UK cross country this year - great fun and much better then expected.

Got to smell the coffee at Banbury and all this fun from a 1000’ winch launch !

Good run to BSE- 5knot climb off tow at 11am to 4000'!
A bit of spreadout as I came back past GRL - but working well - in the middle of the country fantastic, have forgotten how to fly strong thermals to good heights!
But, when is a flight without issues?  Few of mine are.  After going over the Malvern ridge,  it looked OK the other side, but wasn't working as reliably - broken gusty thermals - I need to buy a house at Ledbury as I keep visiting low, but thankfully this time didn't stop.
OK into Hay, a bit low on the run out with M8, but recovered OK and 'boom' when I got back to Malvern and great run back from there.
Speed took a hit for last 50k into and out of Hay. But a nice day out!

Thanks to M8 for the task, never been to Hay-on-Wye before - will upload pictures after eating!

Lovely run to Bury - slog into 20 knot wind to Newark, but OK with 5K+ base and 3-5 knot climbs. Went about half way down the second leg, then abandoned as showers cutting off route to Bicester and home - had to dodge and fly through to get back.

Total distance + 65K as don't seem to have been counted for the distance from when I abandoned - hence speed slower then it should be as speed is for whole task inc getting back.  
Speed also stuffed by the messing around to get back.
Still a nice day out!
Slight cock up with LX9070 declaration in that the 2nd TP flown was the as briefed (BUG}  and not GRM.
Nice flight to start the season.
Thank you Wendy for the task.

My first XC flight of the season, along my favourite airfield-hopping route, which gave me the confidence to keep making progress, despite being unable to climb much above 3000' AGL the entire task. I had declared another turnpoint, but the flight was worth more points without it and undeclared.
Excellent conditions, although a bit of shower dodging needed around Sheffield.

First XC this year.  Cloud base steadily inproved as went north and lowered again after Peterborough on the return.
A bit cold but all in all a good start to the season.
Ran out of ideas and sank between NMK.
Enjoyable flight.  Plenty of gliders about especially to the west.  A bit soft on the way back between Edge Hill and Cranfield.
Spent a long time in the front near GRL so lost the will to complete.
Excellent conditions.
Good conditions until I got downwind of the wash.

Maily soft thermals in East Angli, interspersed with a few good ones.  Approaching Littleport the sky clagged over and I was lucky to get home.  Thanks to Andy Beatty for marking the thermal that got me home.
Pleasant meander around the sky.  Strong winds so no bugs to wash off.

Completely blue with no clues as to where the thermals were but they were going to a good height.  Detoured to Banbury on way back after a longish glide with nothing - the town delivered!  Judging by the numbers of gliders around I think I encountered a competition.
Tricky blue until approaching Corby then really enjoyable conditions under the cu.
Not the day I planned for, or expected.  The strategy was simply to go where it looked soarable, and hope.  Rarely saw the sun.
I spend an additional few hours around Cambridge to complete a 5 hour flight.
Turned out to be a very good day.

I found this challenging. Blue-ish between Didcot and Andover, very good between Andover and Great Malvern, but then progressively more difficult getting home under increasing spread-out. Three 25 km glides under flat murk, topping up in the few sunny patches, finally got me home. I was very pleased to get round!
Thanks to Robert Theil for the task.
Sea air got me at Bedford I think.

Fun flight to Southwold. I had been thinking of the seaside as one option, and David M's suggestion tipped the balance - thanks! There were enough streets and strong climbs for 110 kph by the time I got to Bury on the return, at which point I fell out (the front?) and it took me a while to get going again, after which I was able to enjoy a very strong climb dry which got me most of the way home.
A romp along the streets. She goes well with water! With 899 on his task - thanks.

Awesome to Peterborough under thin cu - my few efforts at turning were a waste of time. Further north thermals became broken and hard to core. After several attempts were rewarded with massive sink after one turn, I decided to respond aerobatically to the next one in an effort to stay in the lift, and that worked. By the time I got to Market Rasen it was almost entirely blue and tricky, so I decided to turn that point and head for home. Fortunately the run back was fairly straightford climb and glide with occasional cu.

This is the furthest north on this line from GRL that I have been I think, and far from being grim, the views were magnificent in clear air from 5000' climbs. A most rewarding flight.

Robert Theil gave me this task but didn't even rig himself. Despite being winched into 5 knots, the feeling of possibly being stitched up lasted as far as Blakeney due to slow progress under top cover. However, on the way to Bury it began to open up and street a bit, and the rest of the task had good climbs and a high cloudbase. Thanks Robert!
Tricky.
I think that no water was the right decision.
Stonking in the middle bit between CSW and WEN.

Just after I had decided that even if the weather cleared it would be too late to fly, the sky perked up so I whipped the covers off, stuck in a couple of barrels and launched after all.

Despite flying perpendicular to the wind on the first leg, there were lines to follow and it was relatively straightforwards to Calvert. It became progressively more blue and weak towards Manton, and I started to wonder if I would get back unaided. The turning point was decent climb at Corby. A couple more and a nice energy line along the shores of Graffham Water resulted in a satisfying high cruise home under a sky that was now solidly blue but clear - I could  pick out the club from 25 km.

Despite the short task, this was a rewarding flight, and I was glad to have launched.

Solid blue. OK going out, but slow on the return in the dying day. Had to be patient for once to get back! Thanks to Robert Theil for the task and for the technique of staying in the top fifth of the convection. ;-D
Blue to start with but then better and better towards GRM.
Slow to get going, but should have started sooner. Very nice towards Newbury. Too much cloud at HusBos, but very pleasant high evening thermals towards Bury. Much too late to get there though.
First half was some of the best conditions I have experienced. Coming back from BUG was not so easy, with quite a long section after the Malvern hills under cirrus with no cu.

Was instructing in the morning so didn't start the task until 3pm.
Very good as far as Mendlesham - thanks to Julia Robson for suggesting going there. Softer on the second leg (sea air?) and the day was dying a bit by the third leg. But good fun.
On the third leg some hang-gliderists helpfully marked a couple of climbs. Difficult to formate on though!
Stonking flight with Glendon.
Great flying by Alon. Easy at first, but then more spreadout and difficult at Ramsey. An authentic gliding experience - not all easy!

Submitted wrong logger file last time.  In Brief: Long flight 8hr 46 total.  Slow run up to the North with weak climbs, thermal tops 3,000 to 3,800 max and several low points.  Took some time in Yorkshire sightseeing, as I trained at Rufforth and lived in York.  By then it was 3.30 so abandoned Richmond (earmarked for more sightseeing) and turned at Northallerton to make it a 500k O/R.  Conditions better coming back, but a soft spot near Sleaford forced me to get out the jet.  Reconnected after about 4 minutes and put it away.  But was getting tired/bored struggling on, so fired it up again to get home faster. Put it away again north of Peterborough and found a good climb which put me on glide for home.  What a super day out.

I had planned and declared a 500km - HRL TAL MYN OXF GRL but the day started later than forecast, and the conditions were difficult at the start.  I got to Talgarth about 3pm, hot and sticky, and decided to just do the Talgarth O/R as this was the first time I had been as far as Wales. Fun.

I planned a 600k taking in an O/R to Peterborough at the end, but decided to just declare the 500k GRL - KLY - FRO - GRL. 
In the event the weather co-operated in spades and I got round the 500k quite quickly (for me) and was able to add on the Peterborough bit for 603km in 6hrs 24 mins. 
I get more points for the declared 500, but it is the 600 I am most pleased with - first time in the UK
Declared GRL - DOW - THP - SRE, but very slow on first leg due to low inversion and decided to turn back at Moreton as the day was forecast to end fairly early.
Too many low saves, and finally had to use the engine near Oxford. Altogether much harder than forecast!

The day was not as advertised. Weak low thermals were substituted for strong high ones! I had planned a 500km, but turned for home half way along the 4th leg as the view ahead was high cover complemented by 100% spread out. Still, I love a challenge and got home without the engine for a change!

What an amazing day. For once the weather exceeded the foecast.  The cloud were in all the right places too.  The task was set on the basis of a trip out west to the best weather, combined with  a clockwise circuit of the Brize airspace.
Too a while to gain height to get me across the line and start. Got round the south of cambridge and the headoff to rattlesdean but had to land out in a field near newmarket.
A fligth to check out finding TPs on a task.
DId this flight for my silver distance and involved spem climbs to nearly 8000 aml

Got as far as uppwood and then headed off to crowland to join several othes who landed there. Decide that i would not make and if i did it would be an expensive aerotow back. So i landed out at uppwood and went to the back of the Q for a tow back.
Got all the way round this time - first time.  Got a good climb over Earith with good views over the fens. Then to grafham and RTB GRL via Hardwick.
I really hoped to get round this time as was last day of the ralley. landed out in another field east of newmarket,

We all took off late - not a great day got the TP at sackville  went past little staunton and tried to get back to GRL - chickened out over st neots and headed back to Little Stauton, Called base and Martin Aldridge came and aerotowed me out.

9.30 Launch, 10.30 engine start, soared back then tried to catch up the others on this 750K attempt.  Managed 660k. East Anglia was fantastic.  A great flight and at least I get to try again. Well done to the two Gransden Lodge pilots who finished their 750s today.

Declared a big flight (750)  but after a 9.30 launch struggled to stay airborne/ get going and after a couple of hours called it a day and flew a consolation tour of east anglia. The other two kept going to post larger consolation distances but I opted for the ice cream and lie down in the shade. Positives - almost any other 750K attempt should have an easier start and will seem a breeze.

Showers blocked the declared task so I changed in flight to this zig zag up and down the only reliable looking bit of sky.  Enjoyable. Had to dodge showers at Thrapston and glid through quite a bit of dead air at Rushden before connecting back near Graham water.

The best day I have flown on in the UK.  For some reason the IGC file was showing the previous flight task (last Friday) despite navigating me round the task ok.  The second leg was showing 146kph with climbs to 7500ft in East Anglia - another 1000ft to go if you wanted.  Underset - could easily have done another 100k. (Flight was declared on the ground to Martin Whitehead)
The blue forecast didn't apply to East Anglia so I had a very pleasant flight with hardly any other gliders around.

A good day out. After an initial good bit things got bluer around Wantage and then I found it tough going. It all picked up at Bruton although the climbs were elusive and a bit two faced. Enjoyed the second leg as I had not flown that route before ever (I know , where have I been) although I came from round there so enjoyed the sightseeing.
As ever the last bit got very good and we could probably have gone further.  Happy to have thought of this task. It seemed perfect for the day.

Remote start at St Neots. Hard work until Broadway then a bit better until Gt Malvern.  Was really please with the flight. Hit the sea air and another 10kt headwind on final glide so prudence (my co-pilot) made me start the engine at Sandy.

Miserable task...most of it in the blue against the wind under cirrus top cover. Too many exporatory turns...too many detours to wisps that did not deliver on most occasions. At least I managed to get around....hopefully next time will be better...
Epic!
Classic climb and glide day and the task was quite ok for once. Blue in places (as forecasted) but some good climbs every now and then. Good fun when clouds were around, not as much in the blue...

Thanks to James Shaw for the idea of going to Walton on the Naze. Flight resubmitted with logger trace from CAI302 as previous trace didn't have full barometric height info. It did have a declaration.
Cocked up the final glide, hence height penalty
Abandoned due to spreadout

Stonking day! Then it started raining.
Late start due to start of season faffing and runway change, but what lovely conditions to start with. Hit showers halfway down second leg that I judged impassible so a nice glide back. Good start the 2022 season.

So windy… Downwind to Long Sutton was unsurprisingly easy and swift. Got into some trouble with the NOTAMs around Fenland airstrip on the way back upwind and had to divert east to the cu. There were some awesome climbs once you found them, but I didn't fancy another look at the fields in the Fens so decided to go home after Bedford instead.

It was all going so well until SHP… That's a lie, the promised higher cloudbase never materialised, the most of the climbs were hard to centre etc. I was trending lower and lower after each climb, and eventually the field of beans came up to meet me. Should've waited near Edgehill for a proper climb, or at the very least stayed with the <1kts I had instead of going to look for something better just before landing out. Live and learn!

Got to the club late, because of a morning appointment. I was a bit concerned about the overdevelopment shutting things down, so I declared yet again GRL-SHP-GRL. Got caught out by the overdevelopment just before Silverstone - the climbs were weaker and harder the centre, but pushed on to Edgehill against my better judgement. It took a while to find a decent climb near Edgehill - I ended up dumping my water and I was getting ready to land there. Fortunately found an acceptable climb that took me to the turnpoint, and another climb back to cloudbase. The sun was on the ground near Northampton which got me home. Unnecessarily stressful because of the spreadout, but still a nice day out before the hellish temperatures hit us early next week 🔥!

Went for SHP o/r since there's a club trophy for it, and west was supposed to be good. But after Silverstone it went blue, all the climbs were hard to center, and they topped out at 3.3k ft. I spent the rest of the flight in survival mode trying to avoid landing out. Managed to tip-toe to SHP from Edgehill after finding a climb there. The back at Edgehill decided to route to Bicester so that I had somewhere to land out in the blue. From Bicester things got better with some cu over NPT, and I had an easy glide home after a climb. Looking at the trace, I never got super low, but the poor thermals in the blue didn't lend me any confidence to push hard.
It was all going so well until we hit the sea air at Crowland. Thanks for the task HGV!

First XC of the season, so very happy to get around without incident. The day started really with strong climbs when I could find the core. Things got a bit soft towards Belvoir castle, but improved again at Crowland on the way home. Had to dodge some showers on the last leg, but I'd been smort for once and topped up early before the rain!

What a great day! No major low points or other shenanigans. Got myself into a tiny pickle with Daventry airspace and had to divert to the south a bit. Cranfield were helpful as always. Thanks for HGV for company, and 521 for the extra nudge to task a bit further.

I found it pretty hard work, especially into wind towards BIC, but looking at the trace now looks like the downwind leg to Pickenham wasn't that easy either. The best thermal of the day over Ely and a strong street got me home. Thanks 751 for the task.
Hard work - I didn't have time to take any pictures. Thanks to JO for the task!
Turned Crowland for Silver Distance claim but couldn't make it back to GRL in high wind. Landed out at Fenland Airfield. Aerotow retrieve to GRL.
Tough flight returning under overcast.
My first 100km flight, rounded off with the completion of my 5hr silver duration, thereby completing my Silver C
Excessive detour via Duxford due to choosing line of energy downwind of track.
My first 200km flight.
My 2nd successfully completed Cloud Rally flight
Just practising. Still too slow...
Just exploring thermals over the Fens on a very hot day. Climbed to 9000ft amsl!
Quick local task before taking thermals to over 7000ft on a booming day...
Local Practice
My first x-country task - Cloud Rally
My first x-country flight!
Good conditions, but too slow and conservative...
Cloud Rally Day 5. Finally got to speed up in a Junior and beat a few people in Discus's and other higher performance gliders. (2nd completed XC flight)

Cloud Rally Day 6. Sacrificed landing out at Crowland A/F with 8 others and getting an aerotow retrieve for getting further and landing out in King's Lynn turn point in an ok field. Considerably assisted by the handicapped barrel size.
lovely end of season training flight
Last day of the Cloud Rally
First flight from my new home. Also first cross-country for a LONG time. Rather slow but good to get back into it.
Cambridge Cloud Rally - not quite fast enough so did it again!
Second round of the same task done a little faster!
Having underset the original task I decided to do the leg that I had thought about earlier!
Only expected to do a small task and got back so early that I decided to extend it!
Cambridge Cloud Rally
Cambridge Cloud Rally
Cambridge Cloud Rally - landed at Crowland
Very late start due to meetings but a fantastic afternoon!
Made a meal of getting to Shipston, and hit the spreadout there.  Meandered back, thinking I'd go back to GRL, extended it to Bury, then Framlingham.  Then hit the spreadout again on the way home.
First time to Talgarth.
Much better day than I had expected.  Pleased I came out.
Windy and slow!
Way underset - but I had to go out early
Thanks to David Tew for the task.  Good for March.
Thermals were quite broken and it didn't street as much as I would have liked.  Decided not to go back to the coast
Fun flight with 700 and 64.  Faffed on the second half of the last leg, so a bit slower than it should have been.
There aren't enough superlatives for this day.  So the task was somewhat underset.  But great fun.

With 72.  We were going to Thetford first, but there was lots of top cover.  Had to make a dash for the sunshine, low, to get away, then most of the trip under that top cover.  Was above glide to get home, but not with a decent margin, so ironically needed to start the engine when in sunshine with cu in sight.

Got airborne to avoid the line of show showers about to hit Gransden.  Had tasked into East Anglia, but snow showers made that look difficult - so gave it a go anyway.  Dodged the showers to Bury, then it was great so decided to go to the coast.  Then up towards Marham.  Coming back was difficult with much lower cloudbase and dead patches - but made it home.  Fun

This was going to be the same flight as Wendy - but a little after Diss I had a GPS failure (loose antenna connection?) and I turned for home.  Perhaps fortunately, since the remaining TP (Framlingham) was definitely in sea air by then.

On the latter part of the Fakenham Framlingham leg we had both been struggling to get climbs - on thinking about it, we were not connected to the cumulus above us because of the sea air front.

Fun flight though.

Surprisingly tricky at times, especially near Great Malvern (sea air from the Severn?).  Actually got the engine out before running into a thermal (didn't start it), then took a high climb before going back into the TP.

Thanks to 72 for the task, and 700 for the company.

Slow start, but got going after MK.
Fun flight with 700.  Great street on the downwind leg - a little bit marginal at the finish.
High blue, and quite consistent.  Only one reasonably low point - very nice run.
This flight had it all - booming conditions, blue, weak blue, and completely overcast.  Quite challenging!
Declared EDG then Mynd. Found conditions unreliable. Got frustrated so decided to turn back after EDG.

Quite weak and low early on. Started at about 2500 feet so care was needed for a while. Conditions improved, as forecast, beyond Ely and from there cloud base was above 5k with some good climbs. The last 40k was under spread out but not a problem as the embedded cu worked quite well. Pleased to get round the task.

Thanks Graham (841) for the task. A good run to EDG. From there is was quite soft for much of the leg with occasional good climbs. Same on return but got better further east. Had a really good climb with 205 and 841 just before LYV which got us round the turn and home. A fun day.

The convergence along the east coast happened as forecast. Wonderful scenery and I enjoyed climbing above the convergence clouds. Lost some time at the TP and the last 60/70k was slow as good climbs had become hard to find. A great day out.
A bit late getting to the launch point so declared a 400k. Well underset. A challenging but fun day.

Windy and challenging. Cloud base lowered to the west of GRL but improved beyond SIL. 5000ft base at GRM. The last 65k home was tricky as there were large blue gaps and few cu. Had to take several weak climbs to survive and then at St Neots hit a really good core. A fun flight.

Very turbulent low down. A mixture of super climbs and soft areas. Shared several thermals with 841 and 871. Thanks George for the task. Very happy with a 300k even though the sky still looked good 3 hours after landing

Forecasts suggested conditions would be stronger towards AND, so declared AND-BIC. Good climbs and decent cloud base but had to fly across streets for some of the flight. From BIC to GRL viz was poor and the few cumulus looked scrappy, so care had to be taken. Controllers helpful as always. A good day out for March.

Had a super run to MK, then there was a big hole with a few scraggy clouds, none of which delivered, so had to divert by more than 90 degrees to stay airborne. Then had to divert even further to avoid Hinton. This cost me at least 30 minutes. After that there was a mix of strong thermals and soft patches. I wonder if there was wave influence about. A fun day out.

As forecast it was slow to get away to the south west, and to get back to GRL. Conditions away from GRL OK but not brilliant. Another super, if challenging day. Enjoyed sharing some climbs with 841 and W8
Weak with haze caps early on, then decent CB to 5K with good cores. Better than expected. Started too early, no water and under declared

First flight of the season. The flight really was to check that everything functioned as it should. Declared BIC-HUS. Turned BIC and conditions looked good towards HUS but feet were getting cold so flew back towards GRL via NOS. Played with transponder of this leg and ignored the task. Lakenheath confirmed the transponder was working OK. A good start to the season.
Very slow for first 50k. Conditions good on second leg to Boston. Didn't get strong climbs on way home due to high cover.
Spread-out was expected but fortunately it didn't happen. A super low stress flight. Previous 3 flights ruined by spread-out.

Must have been desperate or bonkers to fly yesterday. Took a winch launch and climbed away slowly with 899 and 871. Difficult getting past Cambridge due to the weak conditions. The controller was very helpful and patient. Eventually go to reasonable looking cumuli south of Newmarket but few delivered. Got a climb to over 5000 feet at BSE. The sky towards my next TP at Harpley looked awful so decided to run back to GRL. Nothing to climb in until back in the circuit at GRL and then turned Grafham Water. Although a short flight it was fun.

Forecasts much less optimistic than 24 hours earlier. Rigged even though the sky didn't look good with lots of spread-out. Took a winch launch and 15 minutes later the sky filled in and it was difficult to find climbs. Got close to deciding to land back but the sky opened up a bit and I started at about 1pm. Going east was quite good but near Northampton it became very soft with conditions cycling quickly. Pushed on a bit and it was OK but I needed to be back at the airfield at 5pm latest so tuned back about 26k from EDG. Really should have tasked to the east. Still, a good day out.

I reloaded this task as I failed to log it as FAI sectors. A good run to BSE and going west until approaching EDG when it went soft in places and care was needed in and out of HAY where I shared some climbs with 700. Only had a couple of really good climbs on the way home which also slowed the speed. Still, a fun day.
Declared BSE-HAY but spread out prevented progress further west, so went north for undeclared flight.

Declared AND-GRM which was a bad decision as it was blue with a strong looking inversion beyond AYL. So, to get something out of the day went to BUG which is a club task. Coming home was tricky again for the last 65k. Slow speed partly due to change of plan in the air.

Had a dental appointment so late getting to airfield. Still declared a 500k that other pilots had set. Didn't have time to fill glider with ballast. Had a super climb on release from the winch launch and set off. Conditions were rather soft and it looked like it was blueing out on track. Near Calvert decided that I was too late and too slow for a 500 so set of for BUG which is a club task. Had a fun flight. With hindsight might well have got round the 500k task as conditions were good for a couple of hours after landing.
Remote start at OAK as high cover over GRL. OK run to BSE and TIB but at NMT on way west thick high cover weakened thermals. Lost the will to continue west of NMT so requested help from Mr. Solo
Blue at the start but beyond Peterborough good cu and good climbs. Has a couple of really solid cores on the last leg. Made a restart and the logger did not detect that. A super day out.

Was tempted to try for a second 750k but with several gliders on 750 attempts ahead of me on the A/T queue decided to go for a 600k. Got quite low north of Peterborough on the first leg. The Wittering controller was most helpful. After that it was a low stress flight with a high cloud base and mainly decent climbs. Not sure what the fire was towards Scunthorpe but was pumping thick black smoke and blackened the sky way down wind, and it left black oily marks on the wings of several of our gliders. The last leg was superb. Well done to Robert (899) and Ollie (JO) for completing their 750's.

Arrived late at GRL so declared a shorter task than 700, 28 and 871. A real mix of conditions. Almost 8/8ths just after start, then just a few haze caps towards Oxford. Good conditions from MK towards THE, then the clouds started to fill in. Amazing help from the Lakenheath controller who put a heavy on hold at 6000 feet until I climbed at THE and was able to move away from Mildenhall's approach. A fun day out even though quite tricky at times.

Quite weak for the first 50k, then conditions improved. West of EDG was mainly blue with the odd haze cap. Getting back from Waterbeach was a struggle and had to take some weak climbs. Throughout the flight had some strong cores but they were hard to find and often the thermals were   very broken.

Had a good run to MEN and back to west of Cambridge despite some spread-out. The clouds were reliable and mostly delivered decent climbs. Then going west clouds often didn't work and there were many large bluish holes, probably wave influenced. Got too low for comfort several times and had to take scrappy broken thermals to survive. Another challenging day but god fun.

Declared GRL-MEN-BUG-NMT-GRL. Almost blue and quite weak to start, then after about 30k good conditions to MEN and west towards Bidford. Had a super climb just before the clag. Tiptoed into the clag for about 15k and it looked dire so turned back. The sky looked good east of GRL so decided to go to BSE to make the flight an undeclared 500k. Took a climb before BSE to 6000 feet which got me round the turn and home with height to spare. Well done Peter (205) for getting to BUG 30 minutes earlier and for completing the task.

A super task set by Robert (Theil). A challenging mix of booming, soft, spread-out, and long glides.
Part way through the flight it looked overset but later we probably could have done an extra 80-100k

A super run to Littleport and on the second leg until about Oxford. From there onwards it became progressively more difficult. The Devizes area had scrappy Cu with weak and broken climbs. Expected a good run home with a strong tail wind . Got impatient with the weak and broken climbs and pushed on when I should have continued climbing. Conditions excellent again approaching MK.

Declared GRL-TIB-LSU-BSE-GRL. Had a decent run to TIB then LSU. Then the spread out towards BSE killed the thermals so decided to return home. Had to divert quite a long way to stay in the air. We expected some spread out but it was much worse than we hoped for.

Unsoarable at GRL until almost 1300hrs. Started at about 3000ft in super conditions for the first 45 minutes with streeting and solid climbs. It then began blueing out and the thermals became tight and broken and most faded well before cloud base. Tough going in and out of SSO. Totally blue for much of trip home but 899 and I spotted haze caps a bit further east and we headed for Grantham. These then disappeared and it was a struggle to find climbs. Took a few very weak climbs and at Huntingdon had a 3kts which got me on glide. On final glide the thermals were very strong. It was a difficult day. Just happy to complete the task

What a brilliant day. These were the best conditions that I have flown in the UK in about 40 years of gliding. The biggest challenge was avoiding the airspace. Took a big detour south on the way home to within about 15k of Oxford to stay in the higher airspace. The Oxford controller was very helpful.
Gransden Lodge cloud rally Funster day 8. Very enjoyable day out and lovely to see so many mates flying.
My first cross country flight of any sort with a downwind dash for silver distance.  A warm welcome and cup of tea at Rattlesden.  Thanks to Stuart for the retrieve.
Day 5 of the Gransden Lodge cloud rally funster class.  Good fun.

late start so reeled in to a 300
nice day out as long as you were above 3500' - bit torrid below that
highest point 7000' at GRM

what fun, unstable strong northerly airflow with intermittent strong thermals and showers ++ ... declared Newtown- Bala and then spent 2 hours a) trying desperately to stay above 3000' and then b) extensively inspecting every  possible landout option around Ruthin (700' agl wheel down, 2 fields picked ) but Nantclwd and Llantisilio were looking good so finally headed off to Bala  and then had to get back into wind ... planning to land out at Nantclwd until I realised they had released the sheep onto the airfield" .. just love flying at Denbigh - but 27 kph is a record even by my dismal standards !!  who knew stubby wings and no engine coud be this much fun !"

Got the most we could out of the day, trying to task as much as possible away from the inescapable top cover. Fabulous views of a sunny Wash. Ably assisted by Tom Brand who was going to fly his LS 4 until I persuaded him to sit in the back !! Things were a little quiet on final glide so wheel down was delayed !

That well known oxymoron a soarable southerly"  ... declared Thetford Blakeney but bailed out of Thetford just after Soham so off to the seaside with 28 for (close) company  ... with blue sky the airmass sort of worked - but today was about a strong southerly plus thick and thin top cover and on the way home wave bars everywhere ! Only normal soaring was 40k into and 40k out of turn  ... the rest was just weird. Nice views of Blakeney but no ice cream."

the joys of reliable blue to 5500 +
3-4 kts everywhere with 5kts+ under the haze caps
nice task for a hot day

for fear and fun - Denbigh delivers,
sea air in the Clwyd valley made  for a tricky start (cloud climb at Ruthin before I was brave enough to go), 1000' agl at Lyn Brenig for a bum squeak ...  then a great day out with two of the three S's ... survival and sightseeing (just don't mention the speed).
