first leg ok into the wind until Langar -  confused showery convection so I got rained on but Langar let me nab a working Cu in their zone while they were on climb up.
Good again until the higher ground when cloudbase came down to less than 4000' and got rained on again with LYR in a horrible hole so abandoned 9km short and came home.
return was generally a romp apart from a 35 k dead glide over rutland water.

great day out with GRL gliders
declared the club  500 (MEN BUG) because as non turbo I wanted to see the sky  en route to the west and not shoot off the the southwest with all the good stuff.
Pretty epic day out - good conditions, spreadout, difficult cores and total grot on the way home
At Pershore on the way out I suggested to Buzz that this was to infinity and we should abandon - a voice said and beyond" so we went to beyond to bloody Malvern - cue second visit to Pershore at 800 with wheel down and field picked - lucky thermal to get back in the game and finally ran out of sunlight at Cranfield who were helpful as always - thanks to Paul Ruskin for coming to get me in the tug (he'd called ATC before I'd even vacated the runway !!) You don't get all this fun with a turbo !"
Nice trip out to the west with Sarah Drury for delightful company (plus she did half of the flying)
spring rinse out - half soft and half pretty good - pandemic made me kinda forget how much fun this is !!

late start to convection - so wobbled out with blueish convection to barely 3000'
sky switched on at around 1430 when at Brill, then a nice day out. Brize very helpful with zone transit. Missed just one climb on the last leg so lost touch with cloudbase and had  to grind back up again slowly to get on glide. Thanks to 700 for the task and 205/M8/700/871/899 for the company.
Left early due to spread out forecast pm.  The right task for the day.  A bit pedestrian given how good it was in the middle of the country but the first hour was just too slow.
Thanks to 700 for the task.
Thanks to BL for the task.
Total spread out and drizzle stopped play at Rougham.  Still a nice day out and many thanks to Simon Smith for the road retrieve.
Sea air arrived at Upwood and I eventually gave up and landed.
The plan was to do a quick 300K in the heat and for once it worked out perfectly.  7000ft, what's not to like.
Left early to avoid the spread out on the way back from MEN.  Sort of worked, I could always see some sunshine.  In the west it was glorious.
A nice task until spread out at the 2nd TP.
I thought I miss-interpreted the weather again when I found very few people at the club.  How wrong I was.

A fantastic flight after a difficult flight yesterday.
Thanks to 700 for the task.
Thanks to 64 for the task.  A bit slow coming back from MEN due to spread out but fabulous streeting in the west.
Hard work and almost gave up at Wendling due to lack of sun on the ground and ability.  Took a westerly track and things improved.  Only good bit was the last leg.
Underset due to a forecast trough that didn't really affect us

First climb after the winch launch was 6kts and I was too lazy to put water on, dohhhhh
Thanks to JO for the task.
Thanks to Bogdan for his perfect task setting.  A short soaring window with a high cloud base.
Too good to land after the underset main task so did the club 100K to finish the season.
Another nice day out, this season doesn't want to end 😀
I came out to do a 500K but got persuaded to try my first 750K.  Worked out well, no difficult bits.  Thanks to 841 for the task!
The forecast for an early finish made me leave too early again under high top cover.  Still, a nice slow flight and definitely worth a launch.
At last a classic soaring day.  Hawk failed but disabling it and enabling it a few minutes later fixed the problem.  Thanks to JR for the task.
Originally added Olney as last TP but top cover made it impossible to reach.
Very good conditions and back before the showers.  The right task for the day.
The good air was late so M8 and I set off and finally reached it at Bicester after a slow 1st leg.  Very good conditions and high cloud base for the rest of the flight.  Thanks to M8 for the task.
What a great day, East Anglia at its best.
Nice flight with VII to the seaside. Left quite early due to the forecast for spread out, 30min later would have helped near the coast.
Took a while to get soarable and I started too early again but when it got going it was excellent.
Much better day than anticipated.  Testing Hawk was the main purpose of the flight.
Thanks to George Knight for the task.
Hard work to Andover but then somebody switched the weather on.
Abandoned task at GRM and managed to get home in survival mode.
Thanks to George K for the task.
Top cover when I started and then areas of spread out but much better in the south.  Another slow flight.  We need some classic soaring days for a change.
A good day out with M8 and W8.  Strong climbs in spite of areas of spread out.
It started so well but from Membury to Devizes it wasn't great and slowed me right down.  Sack the task setter!
A stress free soaring day, more of the same please.  Thanks to 841 for the task.
The weather wasn't as good as anticipated and neither was I.
A very good and unexpected soaring day.  Underset but it is October.  More of the same please.
Left early and going out was hard work, weak and slow.  High cloud base at the Mynd and a better run home with a few over developed soft parts.
Quick 200 Km before a (late) lunch, then off to a Barbeque in the evening ... but I don't normally organise my XC flying around meal-times, honest!
Blue with just enough isolated Cu to be useable on the way out. Excellent conditions west of Bidford, but blue and difficult from Bidford back to Gransden.

After a week of mostly watching the rain fall at Denbigh, good wave on the day I was supposed to be trailering the glider back to Cambridge. Rigged before Chris Gill's excellent 0930 briefing. TRA(G)s mostly open to FL240, and Chris had also got the floor of the Holyhead CTA overhead Lleweni Parc raised from FL145 to FL180. Aetotowed to 3K just in front of the Denbigh ridge, and straight into 2 knots, in what I assume was the tertiary wave. Slow climb to FL130, then pushed forward to the secondary, which delivered up to 4kt. I broke off at FL180 in order to get back on the ground, de-rig and trailer back to Cambridge and still arrive home on the same day ...

Thanks to all at Lleweni Parc for facilitating a memorable flight.

Surprisingly, I only saw one other glider at Blakeney Point!

Great day out in East Anglia, with superb views of the coast. But next time, I won't task so close to D208.
Nice day out. Well done RASP for forecasting the soarable window that made it possible.

By far the best UK conditions I've ever experienced. Cu cloud bases at up to 10,000 ft, and each one delivering a reliable 6 knots or more - sometimes much more. The main challenge was staying below airspace, especially the corridor between Northampton and Banbury where the base of the Class A comes down to only" FL65. I had to crack open the airbrakes at 100 kt going both ways to get underneath it. No transponder
File created on: so had to break off one climb just below FL100. If it wasn't for airspace
column87: I'd have been on final glide at Little Rissington. Stunning views."

Notes: Very good conditions east of Ely, blue and less good further west.

Vaguely ok until TP2 (Long Sutton) when it became apparent that getting home via the final TP (BSE) was likely to be problematic, so abandoned task. Back tracked to get climb to 5000', but then flew through rain and sustained sink, so diverted again. Found climb from 1500' near Ely that got me home. Scored as undeclared task.
The Club Class were being tested today ...
First flight of 2022 so it was good to get airborne again :-)
Enjoyable afternoon flight with 700 on the same task. 1st half aided by clouds - 2nd half blue, but good blue.
I was intending to do a second lap, but it went all a bit soft at LLE and to the south. Cadar Idris was spectacular as ever.
An enjoyable flight - accompanied Robert Theil on his set task. No real issues - had a bit of a fumble near Wisbech and on final glide before the cu ran out.

A slow start to the day with scrappy blue thermals. However, once clouds appeared the conditions were significantly better than expected. A climb peaking at 6 kt average near Honington from 1400' to 5700' made light work of getting home.
Difficult first leg. Then conditions improved, but the sky never looked inspiring.

Late blue start with low and dfficult thermals on first half of first leg. Good conditions to GRM with cloudbase of 6000' around Gloucester rapidly reducing to 3000' at Malvern. I thought I might run out of day, but there were good climbs coming home, but often quite far apart. Enjoyable flight :-)
I forgot to modify the task in my LX prior to take-off. However, task was agreed and declared verbally at launch point.

I was going to task MEN-BUG, but for something different declared LIT-TAL. I was cautious going into TAL because of the wind strength and potential for bad air. The conditions around TAL were excellent with ~5000' cb.

Delayed start due to the Jubilee airspace restrictions. Very strong climbs around (7kts near Newbury) despite the spread-out. I struggled to find a good climb on the final leg from CAL, but eventually got on to glide.
I had work commitments in the morning so only tasked 300km, but back at 4pm so clearly underset :-(

I was instructing at Cambridge GC in the morning, but then flew 300km afterwards.
Ballistic first leg to GRM at 147 kph. I pushed too hard on return leg ang got too low for the day that costs me some time.
A great day/afternoon to fly :-)

Declared  BUG O/R. Conditions deteriorated beyond Pershore from not very good to bad and arrived at GRM requiring a climb. I flew down the the Malvern hills expecting to find a blue climb, but nothing. Wind was 8kts southerly which was a bit of clue - sea air had come up the Severn valley. In the end abandoned the ridge and eventually climbed away from 700' above Malvern Show Ground. Rounded GRM and came home. Should have done Finn's task up to the Mynd :-(

Another challenging flight. Rewarded with good condtions for the last 35k in/out from the Mynd. Difficult around Droitwich/Worcester area. Once around Shipston I had good run home via Northampton with a solid climb that got me onto final glide. A straight forward day would be very welcome ...
A very pleasant afternoon flight. Conditions were unexpectedly good for October. With hindsight 300km was probably on - helped by having an earlier launch.

Thought I'd take it easy and not bother as the forecast wasn't great, but by 11 am I was so tempted by the look of the sky and decided to come to the airfield (also had to cancel the Sunday plan with the mother-in-law last minute but hopefully she'd understand!) Arriving late had its own advantage that I just watched where all the pundits had gone and made a mini version of their tasks. Strong lifts at places but they were a bit tricky to centre. Nevertheless it was a great feeling completing another task without much stress. I think the P2 enjoyed it too!

First time going that far into the Southwest so I'm pretty pleased to get around. Going out the cloud base was barely at 3000' but the thermals were more or less consistent so off I went. I've been playing safe and topping up whenever I can knowing there aren't many landable airstrips on route (Thanks to David T and Wendy H for pointing out one on the map! Quite reassuring to spot it from the air). Conditions sort of got better on the way back but somehow I always fell out of the thermal after a couple of turns but luckily no drama throughout the flight. Quite an enjoyable day out!

Finally my first 300k! Also the first XC flight since the little one was born 7 weeks ago and it was just lovely to get out of the house and had sometime alone for myself. The glider felt a lot lighter! Going to Bury was the trickier part - just couldn't quite connect with the cloud base and got low at one point. Luckily I ran into a strong lift and managed to be back in the game. I was starting to think I set the wrong task since I had to jump across the streets several times, but after turning Bury things got a lot easier so I pushed on. The rest of the task was straightforward. Great to be on final glide 50km away from home.
Cloud Rally Day 3. Tricky getting out especially around Rattlesden. The blue actually worked more reliably than Cu today!
Cloud Rally Day 4 - another difficult day. Ended up landing out only 10km from home (the clouds looked nice but it was just sink all the way...)
A slow but relaxing flight. Hesitated for too long in the blue in the first leg. Only got going once the clouds started to drift in from the west and the rest was easy and enjoyable.

First XC of the year and I'm really pleased to get around while carrying a 7-month passenger :) Going to Rushden was a race between me and the shower and I had to retreat to Sackville thinking of landing out. Luckily I managed to thermal away and carried on the task to Ramsey and Crowland which was much more enjoyable. Cloudbase was nearly 6000' near Crowland and that helped to get me on final glide as showers again killed much of the activity on the way home. Massive thanks to Steve and Robert T for rigging and de-rigging the glider for me, and others at the airfield who assisted with the ground handling while I'm temporarily out of action for heavy lifting. My special P2 seemed to enjoy the flight too!
Last day at the cloud rally - great fun racing around finally!

Not sure how many more XC flights I can still do this season so I'm really pleased to complete another one now with the 8-month passenger. Going out to MEN was a bit tricky with unreliable thermals and low cloud base. It looked iffy when I turned Mendlesham after losing quite a bit of height under some grey murky clouds, and had to consider diverting to Rattlesden. Luckily by that point the sun came through and big white fluffy clouds were waiting for me on the way home. The rest was easy with 5000' cloud base and strong climbs. Flew past my house in South Camb which is quite nice to see from the top as I always wanted to figure out how many solar panels are there on the roof. Thanks Julia and Ed for doing the same task and offering mutual retrieve.
